name: Elbmesh Given When Then Scenario Tests
summary: Write Elbmesh tests as event-sourcing scenarios: given Resource Events or journal state, when an Action, Reaction, retry, Projection, or Query runs, then assert Events, Receipts, typed errors, journals, or Views.

rules: Write failing tests before production behavior.
Prefer Given Events -> When Action -> Then Events or typed error.
Assert metadata, schema versions, Receipts, Action Journal, Reaction Journal, Operation Journal, View, or Query effects when relevant.
Add architecture-rule tests when a rule is automatable.
constraints: Do not implement production behavior beyond minimal test scaffolding in the test-writing step.
Do not use string matching when a typed error code or structured detail is available.
Do not mix execution failures into Resource Event streams.

items: Given Resource Events -> When Action -> Then Resource Events and Receipt.
Given Resource Events -> When Action -> Then typed domain error and no new Resource Events.
Given operation journal state -> When retry -> Then no duplicate External Operation call.
Given Event -> When Reaction runs -> Then target Action is invoked once and Reaction Journal records outcome.
Given Events -> When Projection runs -> Then View documents and indexes are updated and rebuildable.
Given manifest fixture -> When validate/check_architecture runs -> Then declared relationships pass or fail with named finding codes.

blocks.description: Test writer responsibilities, preferred test shapes, and must-not rules.
ADR for typed Action errors and scenario tests.
Test-first workflow and task card test strategy.
Current Elbmesh scenario and runtime tests.
